WATERS OF HUNTINGBURG, THE

CCN: 155217 · HUNTINGBURG, IN 47542 · Dubois County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
1712 LELAND DR, HUNTINGBURG, IN 47542
Phone
8126834090
Certified beds
95
Avg daily residents
40 (42% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1983-07-15
Setting
Rural
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.36 4.35 2.98 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.74 0.76 0.66 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.57 0.97 0.50 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.04 2.63 1.81 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.31 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.05
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.60
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.71
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
69.2%
annual
RN turnover
57.1%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.54
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.12
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-09-12 13 9 4 56 1 56
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-10-01 21 12 15 100 1 100
Total weighted health score
67.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (46)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

3 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
43 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2026-01-23 2026-02-20
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2026-01-23 2026-02-20
0808 Ensure therapeutic diets are prescribed by the attending physician and may be delegated to a registered or licensed dietitian, to the extent allowed by State law. D 2026-01-23 2026-02-20
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-09-12 2025-10-17
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-09-12 2025-10-17
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-09-12 2025-10-17
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-09-12 2025-10-17
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-09-12 2025-10-17
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2025-09-12 2025-10-17
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2025-09-12 2025-10-17
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-09-12 2025-10-17
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-09-12 2025-10-17
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-07-22 —
0741 Ensure that the facility has sufficient staff members who possess the competencies and skills to meet the behavioral health needs of residents. E 2025-02-05 2025-02-25
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2025-02-05 2025-02-25
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-12-18 2025-01-13
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. E 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-10-01 2024-10-25
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-07-31 2024-08-23
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2024-04-25 2024-05-20
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-02-01 2024-03-06
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. E 2024-02-01 2024-03-06
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-02-01 2024-03-06
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2024-02-01 2024-03-06
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0576 Ensure residents have reasonable access to and privacy in their use of communication methods. E 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. E 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
0882 Designate a qualified infection preventionist to be responsible for the infection prevent and control program in the nursing home. F 2023-07-13 2023-08-04
